---
title: "Introduction to Pandas with National Park Visitation Data (Exercise)"
date: "2024-02-26"
categories: [pandas, exercise]
format:
  html: default
  ipynb: default
code-overflow: wrap
code-fold: show
editor: visual
execute:
  eval: false
---

# <span style="color:green;"> Exercises </span>

## Introduction to Pandas with National Park Visitation Data

<span style="color:red;"> [Solutions](Intro-to-Pandas-NP-Solutions.qmd) </span>

These exercises use National Park visitation data from 1979–2024. For more context about the dataset, see the [data essay](../index.qmd).

**Concepts covered:**

- Selecting columns
- Filtering rows by a condition
- Aggregation (sum)
- Comparing summary statistics across groups

# Load National Park Visitation data

```{python}
import pandas as pd

np_data = pd.read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/melaniewalsh/responsible-datasets-in-context/main/datasets/national-parks/US-National-Parks_RecreationVisits_1979-2024.csv")
np_data.head()
```

# Exercise 1
Select 2 columns from the data.
Save this 2-column dataframe to the variable `smaller_df`.

```{python}
# Your code here
```

How does the number of visits to Washington national parks compare to another state?

# Exercise 2
Filter the dataframe for only values in the state of Washington and save to the variable `wa_parks`.

```{python}
# Your code here
```

# Exercise 3
Calculate the sum total of RecreationVisits to Washington by using `.sum()` on the smaller dataframe `wa_parks`.

```{python}
# Your code here
```

# Exercise 4

Filter the dataframe for only values in another state (your choice) and save to a variable.
Calculate the sum total of RecreationVisits to this state by using `.sum()`.

```{python}
# Your code here
```

How do the number of visits to these 2 states compare to one another?
